stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including transformers, llama, and text-generation.
The content points to the ongoing community interest and benchmarking of leading open-source LLMs.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in large language model applications.
The associated tags confirm its relevance within the transformer and Llama ecosystem.

stories.title: OpenAI just released its answer to Claude Mythos
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: OpenAI has launched 'Daybreak,' a new AI initiative focused on proactive cybersecurity.
Daybreak utilizes the Codex Security AI agent to build threat models from an organization's code.
The system automates the validation and detection of potential vulnerabilities before exploitation.

stories.title: Data center guzzled 30 million gallons of water and nobody noticed for months
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Data centers are consuming massive amounts of water (30 million gallons) without public awareness.
The article questions whether AI itself can solve the growing water consumption crisis within the AI industry.
The outlook presented is pessimistic regarding the industry's ability to manage its environmental impact.

stories.title: EU to crack down on TikTok, Instagram's 'addictive design' targeting kids
stories.author: thm
bullet_points: The EU is planning regulatory action against TikTok and Instagram.
The focus of the crackdown is on 'addictive design' features targeting minors.
This signals a significant regulatory shift regarding social media's impact on youth mental health.

stories.title: Postmortem: TanStack NPM supply-chain compromise
stories.author: varunsharma07
bullet_points: The story details a postmortem analysis of a supply-chain compromise affecting the TanStack NPM package.
This incident highlights critical vulnerabilities in the software supply chain ecosystem.
It serves as an important cautionary tale for developers regarding dependency security.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized for text-generation tasks, indicating its primary function.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing AI workloads for Apple hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs entirely on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI capabilities for coding assistance.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are being brought to users via a partnership with OpenAI and ROOST.
The models are permissively licensed under the Apache 2.0 license, promoting open-source safety tools.

stories.title: ELF: Embedded Language Flows
stories.author: Kaiming He
bullet_points: The paper introduces ELF (Embedded Language Flows) to bridge the gap between continuous and discrete data generation in language modeling.
It addresses the limitation of current Diffusion Language Models (DLMs) operating primarily over discrete tokens, unlike their continuous image counterparts.
The research proposes methods to make continuous DLMs effective with minimal adaptation to the discrete language domain.

stories.title: ELF: Embedded Language Flows
stories.author: Kaiming He
bullet_points: The paper addresses the gap between continuous data generation models (like diffusion/flow-based models) and discrete language tokens.
It proposes 'Embedded Language Flows' (ELF) to make continuous Diffusion Language Models (DLMs) effective for language tasks.
The core contribution is demonstrating that continuous DLMs can be adapted to the discrete language domain with minimal changes.

stories.title: DECO: Sparse Mixture-of-Experts with Dense-Comparable Performance on End-Side Devices
stories.author: Zhiyuan Liu
bullet_points: Introduces DECO, a new sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture.
Addresses the major bottleneck of MoE models: large parameter footprint hindering end-side deployment.
Aims to achieve dense Transformer performance while maintaining low computational cost and small storage overhead.

stories.title: Beyond Red-Teaming: Formal Guarantees of LLM Guardrail Classifiers
stories.author: Luca Arnaboldi
bullet_points: The research addresses the lack of formal guarantees in LLM guardrail classifiers, which currently rely only on testing.
A key challenge identified is the difficulty in formally specifying 'harmful behavior' within a discrete input space.
The paper proposes a novel approach by shifting the verification process from the discrete input space to the classifier's pre-activation layer.

stories.title: DECO: Sparse Mixture-of-Experts with Dense-Comparable Performance on End-Side Devices
stories.author: Zhiyuan Liu
bullet_points: Introduces DECO, a new sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture.
Addresses the deployment bottleneck of large MoE models on resource-constrained end-side devices.
Aims to achieve dense Transformer performance while maintaining low computational cost and small storage overhead.

stories.title: WildClawBench: A Benchmark for Real-World, Long-Horizon Agent Evaluation
stories.author: Yuhang Zang
bullet_points: Introduces WildClawBench, a new benchmark designed to evaluate real-world, long-horizon capabilities of AI agents.
Addresses a critical gap in current agent evaluation by moving beyond synthetic sandboxes and short-horizon tasks.
Features 60 human-authored, bilingual, and multimodal tasks that test agents in native runtime environments.

stories.title: RUBEN: Rule-Based Explanations for Retrieval-Augmented LLM Systems
stories.author: Jarek Szlichta
bullet_points: Introduces RUBEN, a tool designed to generate minimal, rule-based explanations for Retrieval-Augmented LLMs (RAGs).
Employs novel pruning strategies to efficiently distill complex LLM behaviors into a minimal set of governing rules.
Applies these generated rules to enhance LLM safety, specifically testing the robustness against adversarial prompts and safety training.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/NLP tags like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline shown is 'text-generation', indicating a generative AI focus.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or config files.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage Claude Code functionality within Ollama.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary models like Claude.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a working directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives like gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Canvas owner reaches ‘agreement’ with hackers to secure stolen data
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: Instructure (Canvas owner) reached an agreement with hackers to prevent the leak of stolen data.
The breach involved the ShinyHunters hacking group, which claimed responsibility for the attack.
The compromised data volume is significant, reportedly totaling 3.5 terabytes of student information.

stories.title: Yarbo says it will remove the intentional backdoor from its robot lawn mower
stories.author: Sean Hollister
bullet_points: Yarbo is removing a remote backdoor access feature from its robot lawn mower.
The backdoor was a potential security vulnerability allowing bad actors to reprogram the device.
Customers will now have the option to decide whether the feature is installed.

stories.title: Here’s what Mira Murati’s AI company is up to
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Mira Murati's AI company, Thinking Machines, is developing 'interaction models.'
The goal of these models is to enable natural, collaborative interaction between humans and AI.
The technology involves continuously processing multimodal inputs such as audio and video.

stories.title: Linux bitten by second severe vulnerability in as many weeks
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Linux kernel has recently experienced a second severe vulnerability.
The news highlights ongoing security risks within the Linux ecosystem.
Urgent patching is advised, with production-version fixes being released.

stories.title: Starlink shuts down its GPS-style cheat code. Researchers may unlock it anyway.
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Starlink has disabled its proprietary GPS-like location feature.
The removal of this feature is unlikely to stop research into alternative location tracking methods.
The story highlights ongoing academic interest in bypassing or replicating satellite location technology.

stories.title: iOS, macOS, and iPadOS 26.5 updates arrive with encrypted RCS messaging and more
stories.author: Andrew Cunningham
bullet_points: iOS, macOS, and iPadOS 26.5 updates are being released.
A key feature introduced is encrypted RCS messaging support.
These updates are positioned as significant before the next major releases at WWDC.

stories.title: Shepherd: A Runtime Substrate Empowering Meta-Agents with a Formalized Execution Trace
stories.author: Weiyan Shi
bullet_points: Shepherd introduces a functional programming model to formalize meta-agent operations.
It maintains a Git-like execution trace of all agent-environment interactions, allowing state forking and replay.
The system demonstrates high performance, achieving 5x faster process forking than Docker and high prompt-cache reuse.

stories.title: Engineering Robustness into Personal Agents with the AI Workflow Store
stories.author: Wen Zhang
bullet_points: Critiques the current 'on-the-fly' paradigm for AI agents, which relies on rapid, real-time planning.
Argues that the current approach bypasses established, disciplined Software Engineering (SE) processes like rigorous testing and staged deployment.
Proposes a shift toward integrating SE principles to engineer robustness into personal AI agents.

stories.title: DataMaster: Towards Autonomous Data Engineering for Machine Learning
stories.author: Siheng Chen
bullet_points: The paper addresses the bottleneck in modern ML systems: the manual and ad hoc nature of data engineering.
It proposes 'task-conditioned autonomous data engineering' using an autonomous agent to automate data workflows.
The research aims to improve data handling processes, which are becoming critical as model training becomes more standardized.

stories.title: Unmasking On-Policy Distillation: Where It Helps, Where It Hurts, and Why
stories.author: Mehrdad Farajtabar
bullet_points: The paper investigates On-Policy Distillation, a technique providing dense, per-token supervision for training reasoning models.
A key unresolved issue is determining the conditions under which this distillation signal is beneficial versus detrimental.
The research highlights open questions regarding the optimal teacher model choice and the variability of the supervisory signal across tokens.

stories.title: Shields to Guarantee Probabilistic Safety in MDPs
stories.author: Sebastian Junges
bullet_points: The paper addresses the complexity of ensuring probabilistic safety in Markov Decision Processes (MDPs) using shielding techniques.
It introduces a formal framework that conservatively extends classical, deterministic shielding methods to handle probabilistic safety constraints.
This research is relevant to autonomous agents where some level of acceptable risk is permissible, moving beyond absolute safety guarantees.

stories.title: LoKA: Low-precision Kernel Applications for Recommendation Models At Scale
stories.author: Chunqiang Tang
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limited adoption of low-precision arithmetic (like FP8) in large recommendation models (LRMs), despite its success in LLMs.
LRMs are noted as being numerically sensitive, primarily due to small matrix multiplications (GEMMs) followed by normalization.
Direct application of FP8 to LRMs often leads to model quality degradation and extended training times.

stories.title: AssayBench: An Assay-Level Virtual Cell Benchmark for LLMs and Agents
stories.author: Gabriele Scalia
bullet_points: Introduces AssayBench, a new benchmark designed to evaluate Large Language Models (LLMs) and AI agents in biological contexts.
The research focuses on the concept of a 'virtual cell'—a computational model simulating cellular behavior for accelerated biological discovery.
A key application is performing in silico phenotypic screens, where models predict cellular responses to perturbations using mixed textual and phenotypic data.

stories.title: Variational Inference for Lévy Process-Driven SDEs via Neural Tilting
stories.author: Tolga Birdal
bullet_points: The research addresses the intractability of Bayesian inference for Stochastic Differential Equations (SDEs) driven by Lévy processes.
It proposes using Neural Tilting within Variational Inference to model extreme events and heavy-tailed phenomena.
The application has high relevance across finance, climate science, and safety-critical AI.

stories.title: Dynamic Skill Lifecycle Management for Agentic Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Hong Cheng
bullet_points: Addresses the limitations of current Agentic Reinforcement Learning (RL) methods regarding external skill usage.
Proposes a new framework for Dynamic Skill Lifecycle Management, moving beyond assumptions of skill accumulation or internalization.
Focuses on the practical challenge of limited parametric capacity and uneven skill contribution in LLM agents.

stories.title: Optimal and Scalable MAPF via Multi-Marginal Optimal Transport and Schrödinger Bridges
stories.author: Joseph W. Durham
bullet_points: The paper addresses Anonymous Multi-Agent Path Finding (MAPF) on graphs.
It reformulates MAPF as a special class of Multi-Marginal Optimal Transport (MMOT) problems.
The key finding is that under specific conditions, the exponentially complex MMOT problem collapses into a polynomial-sized Linear Program (LP).

stories.title: Equivariant Reinforcement Learning for Clifford Quantum Circuit Synthesis
stories.author: Rob Cornish
bullet_points: The research addresses the synthesis of Clifford quantum circuits using Reinforcement Learning (RL).
A novel approach frames circuit synthesis as an RL problem where an agent reduces a symplectic matrix to identity.
The work introduces an equivariant neural network architecture to aid the learning process.

stories.title: DataMaster: Towards Autonomous Data Engineering for Machine Learning
stories.author: Siheng Chen
bullet_points: Addresses the bottleneck in modern ML where data quality and preparation are manual and ad hoc.
Proposes 'task-conditioned autonomous data engineering' to automate data pipeline tasks.
Aims to create an autonomous agent that improves data workflows, reducing manual effort for practitioners.

stories.title: ELF: Embedded Language Flows
stories.author: Kaiming He
bullet_points: The paper addresses the challenge of applying continuous data generation models (like diffusion/flow-based models) to discrete language tokens.
It proposes 'Embedded Language Flows' (ELF) to make continuous Diffusion Language Models (DLMs) effective with minimal adaptation.
This research aims to bridge the gap between successful continuous generation methods (used in images/videos) and the discrete nature of language modeling.

stories.title: Dynamic Skill Lifecycle Management for Agentic Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Hong Cheng
bullet_points: The research addresses the management of external skills used by LLM agents for complex task solving.
It challenges the assumption that skills must either become persistent guidance or be internalized into the policy.
The work proposes a new approach considering limited parametric capacity and varying skill contributions.

stories.title: RubricEM: Meta-RL with Rubric-guided Policy Decomposition beyond Verifiable Rewards
stories.author: Tomas Pfister
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of training deep research agents where ground-truth rewards are unavailable.
Proposes using rubrics not just for final evaluation, but as a mechanism for policy decomposition and shared interface.
Introduces RubricEM, a Meta-RL approach designed to turn complex, multi-step agent attempts into reusable experience.

stories.title: Grounded or Guessing? LVLM Confidence Estimation via Blind-Image Contrastive Ranking
stories.author: Mohammad M. Ghassemi
bullet_points: Addresses the critical issue of 'visual ungroundedness' in Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs), where predictions rely solely on language priors.
Introduces BICR (Blind-Image Contrastive Ranking), a novel, model-agnostic method for estimating model confidence.
The method specifically aims to distinguish between predictions genuinely informed by the image versus those driven only by textual knowledge.

stories.title: Compute Where it Counts: Self Optimizing Language Models
stories.author: Mohamed S. Abdelfattah
bullet_points: Addresses a limitation in current LLM inference optimization: static computation budgets fail to account for varying token difficulty.
Proposes a novel approach: dynamic budget allocation, where the model learns how much computation to spend per token during autoregressive decoding.
Focuses on improving efficiency by avoiding over-computation on easy tokens and under-computation on hard tokens.

stories.title: DGPO: Beyond Pairwise Preferences with Directional Consistent Groupwise Optimization
stories.author: Wei Wang
bullet_points: Introduces DGPO (Directional-Groupwise Preference Optimization) to improve LLM alignment.
Addresses limitations in current preference optimization methods regarding directional consistency and reasoning diversity.
DGPO aggregates supervision signals at the group level and uses multi-candidate comparisons for direction-aware alignment.

stories.title: Popular Go library fsnotify raises supply chain alarms after maintainer access changes
stories.author: socket.dev via knedl
bullet_points: The popular Go library fsnotify is facing supply chain security concerns.
The alarms stem from recent changes in the library's maintainer access.
This highlights ongoing risks related to dependency management in open-source software.

stories.title: Gemini CLI: Google's Free AI Agent for Your Terminal 🚀
stories.author: dvalin99
bullet_points: Google has released a Gemini CLI tool.
This tool functions as a free AI agent accessible via the terminal.
It represents an integration of advanced AI into developer workflows.

stories.title: Run Claude Code Locally for Free with Docker Model Runner
stories.author: pradumnasaraf
bullet_points: The article discusses running Claude Code locally.
It utilizes Docker Model Runner for local execution.
The primary appeal is running the powerful AI tool for free.
